message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>School reopened in August-September 2020 and their effect on COVID-19 infections is unclear.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We examined Coronavirus Disease-19 incidence following school reopening in Florida.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We found that counties teaching physically had 1.2-fold incidence increase in elementary schools and 1.3-fold increase in high schools, while counties teaching remotely had no increase.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Our results suggest that counties teaching physically could consider teaching remotely, especially in high school, until it is safe to teach physically.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What was known</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Schools reopened in August-September 2020, with some teaching remotely, since the effect of physical reopening on COVID-19 infections is unclear.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What we added</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>counties teaching physically had 1.2-fold incidence increase in elementary schools and 1.3-fold increase in high schools. This suggests that counties teaching physically could consider teaching remotely instead, especially at the high school level.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 Infections Following Physical School Reopening

reference.unstructured: Auger KA , Shah SS , Richardson T , et al. Association between statewide school closure and COVID-19 incidence and mortality in the US. JAMA. Published Online 2020.
Pediatrics AA of. COVID-19 planning considerations: guidance for school re-entry. Retrieved July. 2020;7:2020.
